prompt: Create three synthetic 2-port S-parameter networks representing a connector, a PCB trace, and another connector. Use 201 frequency points from 1 GHz to 10 GHz. The connectors should have S11=0.1, S21=0.9, S12=0.9, S22=0.1. The trace should have S11=0.05, S21=0.85, S12=0.85, S22=0.05. Cascade all three using cascadesparams and plot S21 in dB of the individual trace and the cascaded chain on the same figure.
expected_output: Code creating 3 sparameters objects, cascading with cascadesparams(s1, s2, s3), plotting S21 comparison using rfparam.

prompt: Create a synthetic DUT (2-port, S11=0.2, S21=0.7, S12=0.7, S22=0.2) and two fixture networks (S11=0.05, S21=0.95, S12=0.95, S22=0.05) over 500 frequencies from 100 MHz to 6 GHz. Cascade fixture1 + DUT + fixture2 to simulate a measurement. Then de-embed both fixtures using deembedsparams to recover the DUT. Verify round-trip accuracy by comparing recovered S21 to original S21 and print the maximum error.
expected_output: Code using cascadesparams to build measured chain, deembedsparams(measured, fix1, fix2) to recover DUT, computing max error with rfparam.

prompt: Create two synthetic 2-port S-parameter networks on the same 100-point frequency grid from 1 GHz to 5 GHz. Cascade them using cascadesparams and verify the result is a valid 2-port sparameters object. Print the number of ports and frequencies.
expected_output: Code using cascadesparams producing a 2-port result, verifying size of Parameters is 2x2xN.

prompt: Create two 2-port networks A and B on the same frequency grid and cascade them into AB = cascadesparams(A, B). Then de-embed only the left side (A) by passing an ideal thru for the right fixture: deembedsparams(AB, A, thru). The ideal thru has S11=S22=0, S12=S21=1. Verify the recovered network matches B within 1e-10.
expected_output: Code constructing an ideal thru sparameters object, calling deembedsparams(AB, A, thru), and checking max error < 1e-10.

prompt: Create two 2-port networks A and B on the same frequency grid and cascade them into AB = cascadesparams(A, B). Then de-embed only the right side (B) by passing an ideal thru for the left fixture: deembedsparams(AB, thru, B). The ideal thru has S11=S22=0, S12=S21=1. Verify the recovered network matches A within 1e-10.
expected_output: Code constructing an ideal thru, calling deembedsparams(AB, thru, B), and checking max error < 1e-10.

prompt: Create two 2-port S-parameter networks on DIFFERENT frequency grids (one from 1-5 GHz with 100 points, another from 2-6 GHz with 200 points). Try to cascade them with cascadesparams and show that it fails due to frequency mismatch. Then fix the problem by interpolating both to a common overlapping frequency grid using rfinterp1 before cascading.
expected_output: Code demonstrating the frequency mismatch error, then using rfinterp1 to align frequencies before successful cascadesparams call.
